response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with ~18 years of software development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, Selenium training, MCP code generators). However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of formal people management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing people, running one-on-ones, owning team roadmaps, or leading organizational change. Mobile experience exists but is dated and thin. He would be a credible fit for a senior/staff IC role on this team, but not for the manager position as described.
strengths: 18+ years of broad engineering experience, well above the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tools background: test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup, testing workshops, and AI/MCP tooling
Craftsmanship focus (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development) aligns with an engineering-productivity mission
concerns: No formal people-management experience — the core requirement of the role
No demonstrated strategic roadmap ownership or organizational-change leadership
Mobile-platform experience is limited and nearly a decade old (iOS/Apple TV, 2015-2016)

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure depth
Unclear technical leadership scope (mentoring/founding, but no team leadership record)
